description: A soup made from slowly caramelized onions and stock, traditionally finished with toasted bread and melted cheese on top. It is known for a deep savory-sweet flavor.
preparation_steps: Slice onions and cook slowly until deeply caramelized.
Deglaze the pot with wine or another liquid if using.
Add stock and seasonings, then simmer to develop flavor.
Ladle into oven-safe bowls and top with toasted bread and cheese.
Broil until the cheese melts and browns lightly.

health_advice.benefits: Onions provide flavor and some beneficial plant compounds, and the soup can be filling without being overly heavy.
health_advice.risks: Sodium can be high, especially in restaurant or packaged stock-based versions. Cheese and butter increase saturated fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use a flavorful lower-sodium stock and keep the cheese portion moderate.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions often use beef stock and bread. Vegetarian versions are common, while vegan and gluten-free versions require substitutions.

allergens: Possible soy or celery in commercial stock
